text: The report examines the changes in language use patterns of the U.S. Hispanic population aged five and over from 1980 to the present. The report covers the fifty states and the District of Columbia and examines changes in language use patterns over time as well as the growth of the population that speaks English well. The report also includes a demographic and economic profile of the Hispanic population in the United States and explores the trends in selected socioeconomic attributes of this population over the last forty years.
